clues: Earliest known descriptions of various types of this appeared in Akkadian texts around 2000BC
Due to intense stigma in the 19th century, specialized farms or 'colonies' were greated in Europe and the US to provide safe spaces for individuals affected by this to live and work
Initially thought to be contagious through breath or touch, Hippocrates challenged the notion that this was a manifestation of divine punishment or possession
First synthesized in 1846 as a highly unstable explosive, dynamite usually consists of this among other inert material
Because the substance is absorbed through the skin, dynamite factory workers suffered severe headaches
The same vasodilation that causes headaches is also the mechanism of action for this drug's medical use
Although commonly associated with a bacterial infection, interestingly, people with blood type A also have a higher risk of developing this
This is relatively uncommon in the United States, but rates are high in East Asia leading to widespread national screening programs
In the 20th century, introduction of refrigeration reduced reliance on salted and smoked foods, lowering the risk of this disease in many countries
First isolated from raisins in 1747 by a German chemist, frequent monitoring of this is required for some patients
Also known as C6H12O6, the current term is derived from a greek word referring to sweet wine
Critical for creating ATP, the brain relies on this as its primary energy source
In the 17th century, the bark of the cinchona tree from South America was introduced as a powerful treatment for this because the bark provided a natural source of quinine
This disease famously affected George Washington and Abraham Lincoln, but is no longer endemic in the United States
The name derives from Italian for 'spoiled air' or 'bad air' reflecting the belief that this disease was caused by vapors from swamps
